title: The Best Free AI Tools for Developers in 2025
description: A curated list of the best free AI tools for developers — from code assistants to image generation to API testing. All free or have generous free tiers.
author: MyAppAI Team
body:
AI tools have become essential for developers in 2025. The good news: many of the best ones are free or have generous free tiers. Here's the curated list — no fluff, just tools that actually save time.

## Code Assistants

### GitHub Copilot
The most widely used AI code assistant. Autocompletes code, explains functions, and generates tests. Free for students and open-source maintainers, $10/month for individuals.

**Best for:** Autocomplete, boilerplate generation, test writing

### Cursor
An AI-native code editor built on VS Code. The free tier includes 2,000 completions/month and 50 slow requests. The Pro tier ($20/month) is worth it for heavy users.

**Best for:** Codebase-aware chat, multi-file edits, explaining complex code

### Codeium
Fully free AI code assistant with no usage limits. Works in VS Code, JetBrains, Vim, and more. Not as powerful as Copilot but the price is right.

**Best for:** Developers who want free autocomplete without limits

## AI APIs (Free Tiers)

### Google Gemini API
Generous free tier: 15 requests/minute, 1 million tokens/day with Gemini 1.5 Flash. Great for prototyping.

**Free tier:** 1M tokens/day with Flash model

### Groq
Incredibly fast inference (often 10x faster than OpenAI) with a free tier. Great for real-time applications.

**Free tier:** 14,400 requests/day, 6,000 tokens/minute

### Ollama
Run LLMs locally on your machine. Free forever, no API key needed. Supports Llama 3, Mistral, Phi-3, and dozens more.

**Best for:** Privacy-sensitive apps, offline development, cost-zero prototyping

```bash
# Install and run Llama 3
ollama pull llama3.2
ollama run llama3.2
```

### Anthropic Claude
Free tier available at claude.ai. API access requires payment but the web interface is free for personal use.

**Best for:** Long documents, nuanced reasoning, coding tasks

## Image Generation

### Stable Diffusion (Local)
Run image generation locally with ComfyUI or Automatic1111. Free forever, unlimited generations.

**Best for:** Developers who want full control and no usage limits

### Ideogram
Free tier with 10 generations/day. Excellent at text-in-image generation.

### Canva AI
Free tier includes AI image generation, background removal, and design tools. Great for non-designers.

## Developer Productivity

### Perplexity AI
AI-powered search with citations. Free tier available. Better than Google for technical questions because it synthesizes answers from multiple sources.

**Best for:** Research, finding documentation, understanding new technologies

### Phind
AI search engine specifically for developers. Free, fast, and understands code context.

**Best for:** Debugging, finding code examples, Stack Overflow alternative

### Warp Terminal
AI-powered terminal with natural language command generation. Free tier available.

```bash
# Type in natural language, Warp converts to command
"find all files modified in the last 7 days"
# → find . -mtime -7 -type f
```

## Testing and QA

### Postman AI
Postman's free tier now includes AI-powered API testing, test generation, and documentation.

### Playwright with AI
Microsoft's Playwright testing framework has AI-assisted test generation. Free and open-source.

## The Stack I'd Recommend

For a solo developer or small team:

1. **Code:** Cursor (free tier) or Codeium (fully free)
2. **AI API:** Groq for speed, Gemini for volume, Ollama for local
3. **Search:** Phind for technical questions
4. **Images:** Stable Diffusion locally or Ideogram free tier
5. **Terminal:** Warp

Total cost: $0/month to start. Upgrade to paid tiers when you're generating revenue.

## A Note on Free Tiers

Free tiers exist to get you hooked. That's fine — use them to build, validate, and launch. Once you're making money, pay for the tools that save you the most time. The ROI on good developer tools is almost always positive.
